title: A Chronicle of London, from 1089 to 1483
summary: CME00006 is a selective digital encoding of Nicolas and Tyrrell's 1827 compilation. That edition prints an anonymous London chronicle from Harley MS 565, collates and continues it from Cotton MS Julius B I, and appends poems and documents from several witnesses and earlier printed sources. The complete 1827 book and several supporting editions are cached locally. No complete public facsimile was found for any of the four manuscript codices; the only public manuscript images found are an 1827 lithographic facsimile of Harley MS 565 f. 37 and a modern cropped detail of f. 68r. Future reproduction requests should ask for each complete physical codex, then associate the encoded works with the folio ranges recorded here.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00006
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The XML identifies itself as CME00006 in IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID.
The source submodule is a distinct repository; this lineage record does not modify that artifact.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00006
entities.description: Public Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of selected portions of the 1827 edition.
notes: The encoding includes the title page, printed pp. 1-147, and printed pp. 216-253, but not the complete 1827 volume.
No item-level statement establishing the original keying/OCR method or the rationale for this selection was found.

entities.label: Nicolas and Tyrrell, A Chronicle of London (1827)
entities.description: The printed compilation immediately underlying CME00006, combining a London chronicle, editorial notes and variants, documentary transcripts, poems, and later historical ballads.

bibliographic.citation: Nicholas Harris Nicolas and Edward Tyrrell, eds., A Chronicle of London, from 1089 to 1483 (London: Longman, Rees, Orme, Brown, and Green, 1827).
bibliographic.title: A Chronicle of London, from 1089 to 1483
bibliographic.publisher: Longman, Rees, Orme, Brown, and Green

notes: The printed title's starting date 1089 is an error; the chronicle begins with 1189.
The complete book contains numbered pp. 1-274. CME00006 omits the dedication and preface, pp. 149-215, and pp. 254-274.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 565
entities.description: A London-produced parchment codex written about 1444. It contains the principal early witness used by the 1827 editors, plus St Paul's tablet transcripts and the two long poems encoded in CME00006.

notes: The complete physical codex includes material not used in CME00006, including the king list on f. 1 and documents on ff. 100v-101v.
Work-bearing portions: St Paul's transcripts ff. 2r-9r; London Chronicle ff. 10r-99r; Henry V expedition poem ff. 102r-114r; Lydgate's Henry VI entry poem ff. 114v-124r.
The British Library record says digital images are currently unavailable. A legacy viewer object also failed during review.
A future facsimile request should cover the whole codex, not only these work-bearing folios.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Julius B I
entities.description: A London paper manuscript whose chronicle on ff. 2r-90v runs from 1189 to 1483. The 1827 editors used it for variants and for the continuation after Harley MS 565 ends.

date_statements.label: Chronicle manuscript
date_statements.value: Last quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: The chronicle occupies ff. 2r-90v; later material on ff. 91v-98v is early sixteenth century.
The exact sub-folio boundary at which the 1827 printed continuation begins has not been established.
No public page images were present in the current catalog record.
A future facsimile request should cover the whole codex and then point to the chronicle on ff. 2r-90v.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Nero A VI
entities.description: The composite Cotton manuscript containing the reconciliation poem printed on pp. 251-253 of the 1827 edition and encoded in CME00006.

notes: The relevant poem is on ff. 196v-197v within the cataloged component ff. 71-197.
The outer edges were damaged in the Cotton Library fire.
A future facsimile request should cover the whole physical manuscript, then identify ff. 196v-197v as the CME00006-associated portion.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 43488
entities.description: A bound volume of Paston Letters and Papers containing John Bocking's letter to Sir John Fastolf on f. 41, ultimately excerpted in CME00006 through Fenn's 1787 edition and the 1827 Chronicle.
notes: The catalog dates the letter 15 March 1458 in modern dating; the historical regnal-year heading is 1457/8.
A future facsimile request should cover the whole bound volume, then identify f. 41 as the CME00006-associated portion.

entities.label: Fenn, Original Letters, volume 1 (1787)
entities.description: The printed source expressly cited by Nicolas and Tyrrell for John Bocking's letter.

bibliographic.citation: John Fenn, ed., Original Letters, Written during the Reigns of Henry VI, Edward IV, and Richard III, vol. 1 (London: G. G. J. and J. Robinson, 1787).
bibliographic.title: Original Letters, Written during the Reigns of Henry VI, Edward IV, and Richard III
bibliographic.publisher: G. G. J. and J. Robinson

entities.label: Kingsford, Chronicles of London (1905)
entities.description: A later comparative scholarly edition used here to check the London-chronicle tradition and Nicolas and Tyrrell's handling of Harley MS 565 and Cotton MS Julius B I.

bibliographic.citation: Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, ed., Chronicles of London (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1905).
bibliographic.title: Chronicles of London

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of A Chronicle of London (1827)
entities.description: Complete page-image scan of the 1827 printed volume, including its lithographic manuscript facsimile and all numbered pages.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Fenn's Original Letters, volume 1 (1787)
entities.description: Complete page-image scan of Fenn's first volume, including the Bocking letter at pp. 154-156.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Kingsford's Chronicles of London (1905)
entities.description: Complete page-image scan of Kingsford's comparative edition.

entities.label: Project Gutenberg eBook 27027
entities.description: A public digital transcription of the complete 1827 book, prepared from a 1995 Llanerch reprint and Internet Archive images of the 1827 edition.

entities.label: Cropped image detail from Harley MS 565 f. 68r
entities.description: A public color crop showing part of f. 68r. It is useful supporting evidence for the chronicle's script and rubrication but is not a complete leaf or a facsimile of the codex.
notes: Coverage is partial even for f. 68r. Do not present this as a whole-manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: 1827 lithographic facsimile of Harley MS 565 f. 37
entities.description: The frontispiece of the 1827 edition reproduces one manuscript page from Harley MS 565. It is available inside the complete Internet Archive scan but does not represent the whole codex.
entities.reproduction_format: Lithographic plate reproduced in PDF page images

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Current general guidance consulted for the duration of copyright in works first published before 1978. Item-specific provider statements remain the primary practical evidence recorded below.

rights.component: The repository XML artifact as a distinct digital file
rights.basis: No file-specific license or rights statement was found in the artifact itself during this review.
notes: The age of the underlying texts and editions does not by itself establish the licensing of this particular XML artifact.

rights.component: The CME00006 digital text and materials presented by University of Michigan
rights.basis: The item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: No Copyright-United States is not an affirmative worldwide license.
This statement must not be generalized to newly supplied British Library manuscript photography.

rights.component: The text and typography of the 1827 printed edition
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1827; U-M applies No Copyright-United States to its copy-derived materials, and Project Gutenberg distributes the book as public domain in the United States.
notes: This does not decide rights in a newly created manuscript photograph or provider-imposed reproduction terms.

notes: Project Gutenberg's trademark and redistribution terms remain distinct from copyright in the underlying book.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two related but non-identical London-chronicle manuscripts
editorial_practices.representation: Harley MS 565 supplies the base through its ending; Cotton MS Julius B I supplies variants and the continuation to 1483
editorial_practices.description: Nicolas and Tyrrell print the Harley chronicle, mark its endpoint on p. 133, continue from Cotton, and record Cotton variants in the text or notes.
editorial_practices.scope: The main chronicle, printed pp. 1-147, and its editorial notes
notes: Kingsford treats these as evolving versions within a wider London-chronicle tradition, not simply one original compiler followed by one continuator.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple contents of Harley MS 565 and additional documentary and poetic sources
editorial_practices.representation: A curated printed compilation rather than a codex-complete transcript
editorial_practices.description: The editors omit Harley's king list on f. 1, transcribe or excerpt selected Harley contents, add a Cotton continuation and variants, and append documents and poems from other witnesses and printed authorities.

editorial_practices.representation: Title page, pp. 1-147, and pp. 216-253 only
editorial_practices.description: CME00006 encodes the main chronicle and selected appended texts while omitting the dedication, preface, editorial notes and St Paul's transcripts, part of the illustrations, the Vespasian variant, and later ballads.
notes: The reason for the selection was not found.
The XML lacks a PB numbered 234 although the text continues across that printed-page transition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed hierarchy, page milestones, and three words absent from the earlier encoding
editorial_practices.representation: Revised XML division structure and supplied missing words
editorial_practices.description: The 2016 revision note records a structural review, title-page proofreading, and the supply of three missing words at PB REF61.
editorial_practices.scope: CME00006 XML revision recorded as 7 January 2016
notes: The schema's closest practice category is selection; the source note describes structural correction rather than a new source edition.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR; EEBO/IDG; title page; chronicle; appended DIV1 elements
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00006 and preserves the 1827 title page, main chronicle, two long Harley poems, a Bocking letter extract, one reconciliation-poem version, page milestones, notes, and the 2016 structural-review statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00006.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights, contents, and full text
evidence.summary: Confirms the title, London 1827 imprint, public text, stable identifier, subjects, and public-domain / No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: Nicholas Harris Nicolas and Edward Tyrrell, eds., A Chronicle of London, from 1089 to 1483 (London: Longman, Rees, Orme, Brown, and Green, 1827), Internet Archive item chronicleoflondo00nicouoft.
evidence.locator: Frontispiece; title page; dedication; preface pp. v-viii; chronicle pp. 1-147; notes pp. 149-190; illustrations pp. 191-274
evidence.summary: Complete primary edition evidence for title, editorial responsibility, principal manuscripts, source selection, collation, printed boundaries, component attributions, and material omitted by CME00006.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 565.
evidence.summary: Catalogs the complete parchment codex, c. 1444, its work-bearing portions, physical extent, origin, and currently unavailable digital images.

evidence.citation: Access checks for the legacy British Library digital object associated with Harley MS 565.
evidence.summary: The legacy viewer did not deliver images, and the associated endpoint returned access denied; this agrees with the current catalog's statement that digital images are unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Julius B I and component ff. 2-98.
evidence.locator: Chronicle ff. 2r-90v; complete codex physical description and access
evidence.summary: Catalogs the paper Chronicle of the City of London, 1189-1483, its London origin, last-quarter-fifteenth-century date, complete physical extent, and absence of digitized content.

evidence.citation: Charles Lethbridge Kingsford, ed., Chronicles of London (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1905), introduction pp. vi-xxiii.
evidence.locator: Introduction pp. vi-xxiii; Harley 565 pp. x-xi; Cotton Julius B I pp. xiii-xv
evidence.summary: Comparative manuscript study establishing the evolving London-chronicle tradition, Harley c. 1443/4 version, Cotton continuation, and the way Nicolas and Tyrrell used both witnesses.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Nero A VI, ff. 71-197.
evidence.locator: Reconciliation of Henry VI with the Yorkists, ff. 196v-197v
evidence.summary: Identifies the exact reconciliation-poem component, its folios, broader codex extent and damage, and physical/request access without public digitized images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 43488, Paston Letters and Papers.
evidence.locator: f. 41, John Bocking to Sir John Fastolf, 15 March 1458
evidence.summary: Identifies the original letter, modern date, sender, recipient, exact folio, 59-folio bound volume, paper support, and currently unavailable digital content.

evidence.citation: John Fenn, ed., Original Letters, Written during the Reigns of Henry VI, Edward IV, and Richard III, vol. 1 (London: G. G. J. and J. Robinson, 1787).
evidence.locator: pp. 154-156 and embedded Google usage guidelines
evidence.summary: Immediate printed source cited by the 1827 edition for Bocking's letter; prints facsimile-style and normalized texts and explains the historical 1457/8 date.

evidence.citation: Project Gutenberg eBook 27027, A Chronicle of London, from 1089 to 1483.
evidence.locator: Header, title, transcriber's note, and complete text
evidence.summary: Identifies Nicolas and Tyrrell as editors, states U.S. public-domain status, notes the erroneous 1089 title date, and explains that the transcription used a 1995 reprint and 1827 Internet Archive images.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons, Manuscript page - British Library Harley MS 565 f. 68r.png.
evidence.locator: File description, original file, and rights statement
evidence.summary: Provides one public cropped color detail from f. 68r and applies the Public Domain Mark to that exact file.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the 1827 Harley MS 565 f. 37 plate and the Commons f. 68r PNG.
evidence.summary: Confirms that the 1827 item contains a single-page lithographic facsimile and that the Commons image is only a cropped detail with red and black script, not a whole leaf or codex.
Provides the online request route and explains that original manuscripts are photographed; reference images may use an existing surrogate unless color is requested.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms and conditions for imaging services, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States that supplied files may not be copied, reproduced, published, or otherwise reused without written permission and that separate permission and fees may apply.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services price list, 2025/26.
evidence.summary: Lists per-image JPEG and TIFF charges and other imaging-service rates; a whole-codex order therefore requires a service quote and may be substantial.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Current general U.S. Copyright Office guidance on copyright duration, used only as context alongside item-specific provider statements.

open_questions.question: Can complete color facsimiles of all four physical manuscript codices be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to check for existing complete color photography before commissioning new work.
For each order, request the complete physical codex, including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and every leaf where available, rather than only the work-bearing portion.
After receipt, keep the whole-codex bundle under source-cache and record image-level checksums or a bundle manifest.
Associate CME00006 with Harley MS 565 ff. 2r-99r and 102r-124r; Cotton MS Julius B I ff. 2r-90v; Cotton MS Nero A VI ff. 196v-197v; and Add MS 43488 f. 41.
Obtain written reuse permission before publishing any supplied images or bundles in the Git repository.
notes: Only two partial Harley images were found publicly: an 1827 plate of f. 37 and a modern crop of f. 68r.
The current BL rate card is per image and does not establish a final whole-codex price.

open_questions.question: Which exact folios of Cotton MS Julius B I correspond to the continuation printed on pp. 133-147 of the 1827 edition?
next_steps: Inspect a complete Cotton MS Julius B I facsimile when obtained.
Align the first post-Harley entry on printed p. 133 and the final 1483 entry against manuscript ff. 2r-90v.
Replace the current broad work-portion locator with the exact start and end folios.

open_questions.question: What keying method and selection rationale produced CME00006, and why is the printed p. 234 milestone absent?
next_steps: Check U-M project documentation or contact Special Collections Research Center staff for production notes.
Compare the XML around PB REF233 and REF235 against the complete 1827 scan.
Record whether any unmarked text loss or only a milestone omission occurred.

open_questions.question: Can the anonymous Henry V expedition poem in Harley MS 565 ff. 102r-114r be assigned a more precise conventional title or attribution?
next_steps: Consult the manuscript's cited secondary bibliography and standard Middle English verse indexes.
Keep the current descriptive title and anonymous responsibility unless a reliable authority establishes more.

review_notes: This record models the 1827 book as a compilation, not as a single medieval work or a transcript of one codex.
Physical manuscripts, printed editions, digital encodings, and facsimiles remain separate entities even when the same passage connects them.
Local-cache coverage is stated relative to the entity represented: a complete printed-book scan may still be only partial evidence for a manuscript witness.
Whole-manuscript acquisition is the default for future facsimile requests; work-level associations are carried by folio locators rather than by trimming the acquired bundle.
